critical.mandate: Read ONLY frontmatter + Findings section from story files — never load full content (token budget protection)
Every debt item must be classified, scored, and aged — none may be left unprocessed
Dashboard is a living document — overwrite on each run, preserve trend data from previous version
Each debt item gets a stable ID (TD-1, TD-2, ...) assigned in Step 2. This ID MUST be preserved across ALL subsequent steps — never renumber items when sorting or filtering.
For each triaged item, validate the target story status. Flag items where the target is done (STALE TARGET) or missing (UNASSIGNED).

step.title: Scan Debt Sources
step.action: If {implementation_artifacts}/tech-debt-dashboard.md exists: load it for trend comparison (previous totals, previous items list)
Read {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml — identify current sprint_id and all stories with status 'backlog'
Scan story files in {implementation_artifacts}/ — for each .md file:
1. Read YAML frontmatter (stop at closing ---) to get story key, status, sprint_id, type
2. Scan for ## Findings section — collect rows where Type = 'tech-debt'
3. Also collect findings of Type = 'bug' with Severity = 'medium' or 'low' that are NOT marked [TRIAGED] or [DISMISSED]

For each finding marked [TRIAGED → {target_key}], validate the target story: 1. Check if {implementation_artifacts}/{target_key}-*.md exists 2. If exists: read its frontmatter status field 3. Determine resolution status: - Target status = 'backlog' or 'validating' or 'ready-for-dev' → QUEUED - Target status = 'in-progress' → IN PROGRESS - Target status = 'review' → IN REVIEW - Target status = 'done' → STALE TARGET (story completed but debt still exists — finding was likely added after implementation) 4. If target file doesn't exist → UNASSIGNED 5. If target is not a valid story key (e.g., "cleanup", "later", free text) → UNASSIGNED

For each STALE TARGET item: verify whether the debt was actually resolved by the target story. 1. Read the finding description — identify what specific file, config, or pattern the debt refers to 2. Check the filesystem: does the issue still exist? (e.g., if finding says "duplicate file X", check if both copies still exist; if finding says "missing skill Y", check if it was created) 3. If the issue is RESOLVED (no longer exists in filesystem): mark as RESOLVED and exclude from classification/scoring 4. If the issue PERSISTS despite target story being done: keep as active debt with status STALE TARGET 5. Report: "{N} STALE TARGET items verified: {resolved_count} resolved (excluded), {persists_count} still active"
Separate items into two pools: (a) untriaged findings still in story files, (b) triaged debt stories already in backlog

If untriaged findings exist: include them in the analysis with Resolution Status = UNASSIGNED. Do NOT recommend running /gaia-triage-findings here — triage may have already run and these items fell through its filters. Flag them for Step 6 recommendations instead.
If no debt items found at all: inform user "No technical debt detected" and stop

step.title: Classify Debt
step.action: Before assigning IDs, detect and merge duplicate findings that describe the same root cause: 1. Compare all items — two items are duplicates if they describe the same root cause: - Same file/config/pattern referenced - Same type of issue (e.g., "dual directories" from different source stories) - One finding is a subset of another 2. For each group of duplicates: merge into a single item - Use the most descriptive title - Combine all source stories (e.g., "E8-S2, E8-S4") - Keep the same target story - Mark as "(merged from {count} findings)" in the Item column 3. Report: "Merged {N} duplicate findings into {M} items"

Assign each MERGED debt item a stable ID: TD-1, TD-2, TD-3, etc. IDs are assigned AFTER merging so they reflect the final deduplicated list. This ID stays with the item through ALL subsequent steps — never renumber.
Categorize each debt item into exactly one category:
DESIGN — architectural shortcuts, missing abstractions, tight coupling, violation of design patterns
CODE — code smells, duplication, excessive complexity, naming inconsistencies, SOLID violations
TEST — missing tests, flaky tests, low coverage areas, manual-only testing
INFRASTRUCTURE — CI/CD gaps, missing monitoring, manual deployment steps, environment drift
Tag each item with its source: 'finding' (from dev-story), 'triage' (backlog story from triage-findings), 'manual' (user-reported)

If any items have resolution status STALE TARGET or UNASSIGNED, present a warning block BEFORE the classification table: ⚠ Target Validation Issues: - TD-{N}: Target {key} is DONE — finding was likely added after implementation. Needs re-triage: create a new story or dismiss. - TD-{N}: Target "{text}" — no valid story assigned. Needs triage. Recommend: run /gaia-triage-findings to reassign these items.
Present classification table with ALL columns: | ID | Item | Category | Source | Target Story | Resolution Status | Resolution Status values: QUEUED, IN PROGRESS, IN REVIEW, STALE TARGET, UNASSIGNED
Present category summary table grouped by category with counts

step.title: Score and Prioritize
step.action: For each debt item, assess three dimensions (1-5 scale):
Impact — How much does this affect development velocity, reliability, or user experience? (1=minimal, 5=severe)
Effort — How much work to resolve? (1=trivial fix, 2=few hours, 3=1 story, 4=multi-story, 5=epic-sized)
Risk of Inaction — What happens if ignored for 3 more sprints? (1=nothing, 3=slows team, 5=production incident)
Calculate Debt Score = (Impact + Risk of Inaction) - Effort
Map to priority: Score >= 7 = FIX NOW, 4-6 = PLAN NEXT, 1-3 = TRACK
Sort all items by Debt Score descending. Add a Rank column for sorted position, but KEEP the original TD-{N} ID from Step 2.
Present the FULL scored table with all columns preserved: | Rank | ID | Item | Category | Source | Target | Resolution | Impact | Effort | Risk | Score | Priority |
In YOLO mode: auto-confirm the recommended scores for all items — skip the user review prompt below.
step.ask: Review the top 5 scored items — confirm or adjust the scores

step.title: Calculate Aging
step.action: For each debt item, calculate age in sprints:
- Triaged backlog stories: compare story creation sprint to current sprint_id
- Untriaged findings: compare source story sprint_id to current sprint_id
- If sprint_id not available: use file modification date as fallback
Apply SLA thresholds and escalate where needed:
FIX NOW items older than 1 sprint → flag as OVERDUE
PLAN NEXT items older than 3 sprints → auto-escalate to FIX NOW
TRACK items older than 5 sprints → flag for relevance review (may be obsolete)
Build aging distribution: count items per age bucket (current sprint, 1-2 sprints, 3-4 sprints, 5+ sprints)

step.title: Generate Dashboard
step.action: Load template from {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/templates/tech-debt-dashboard-template.md
Fill summary metrics: total count, FIX NOW / PLAN NEXT / TRACK counts, overdue count, debt ratio, STALE TARGET count, UNASSIGNED count
Fill category table: count and oldest item age per category
Fill aging histogram: ASCII bar chart showing items per age bucket
Fill top 10 table with ALL columns: ID, item description, category, source, target, resolution status, impact, effort, risk, score, age, priority, recommended action
Fill overdue alerts: list all OVERDUE and auto-escalated items with source and age
Fill target issues section: list all STALE TARGET and UNASSIGNED items with recommendation to re-triage
Fill trend section: compare current totals to previous dashboard (if exists), compute deltas
If no previous dashboard: show "First review — no prior data" in trend section
step.template-output: Generate tech debt dashboard from template with all metrics, rankings, aging alerts, target validation issues, and trend data.

step.title: Recommend Actions
step.action: Based on dashboard findings, recommend specific next steps:
If any STALE TARGET or UNASSIGNED items exist: "Run /gaia-triage-findings to reassign {count} items with invalid targets"
If any FIX NOW + OVERDUE items exist: "Run /gaia-correct-course to inject these into the current sprint"
If debt ratio > 30%: "Consider dedicating 20% of next sprint capacity to debt reduction"
If TEST debt dominates: "Run /gaia-test-automate to address test coverage gaps"
If DESIGN debt dominates: "Consider /gaia-change-request for architectural refactoring"
If INFRASTRUCTURE debt dominates: "Run /gaia-ci-setup to close CI/CD gaps"
If items were auto-escalated: "Review escalated items — aging has upgraded their priority"
Present the specific /gaia-* command chain for each top-priority item

step.title: Save to Val Memory
step.action:
Auto-save tech debt review decisions to Val's memory sidecar (no user prompt required):
1. Append to {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Tech Debt Review: {total_count} items
- **Agent:** validator - **Workflow:** tech-debt-review - **Sprint:** {sprint_id} - **Status:** recorded
Dashboard: {total_count} items. FIX NOW: {fix_now_count}. PLAN NEXT: {plan_next_count}. TRACK: {track_count}. Overdue: {overdue_count}. Auto-escalated: {escalated_count}. Stale targets: {stale_count}. Unassigned: {unassigned_count}. Debt ratio: {ratio}%. Trend: {delta vs previous} ({up/down/stable}). Top category: {dominant_category} ({count} items). Top 3 FIX NOW items: {brief list with scores}. Recommendations: {list of recommended /gaia-* commands}.
2. Replace body of {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/conversation-context.md (preserve header above first ---): Last session: Tech debt review for {sprint_id}. Date: {YYYY-MM-DD}. Items: {total_count}. FIX NOW: {fix_now_count}. Overdue: {overdue_count}. Stale targets: {stale_count}. Unassigned: {unassigned_count}. Debt ratio: {ratio}%. Trend: {up/down/stable}.
If _memory/validator-sidecar/ directory or files don't exist, create them with standard headers. If writing fails, log warning and continue — memory save is non-blocking.
